chat_history.message:
BRD Document Content:
Business Requirements Document (BRD)
Core Banking Domain Data & Functional Requirements
1. Document Purpose
This BRD outlines the business requirements for the Core Banking domain covering customer onboarding, account lifecycle management, transactions processing, account balances, fees & charges, KYC compliance, and GL postings. The document provides a unified business view leveraging the supplied Core Banking ER diagram to ensure standardized data, seamless processing, and accurate financial reporting.
2. Business Background
Banks operate critical core systems to handle customer information, accounts, balances, transactions, fees, and ledger postings. Current systems often involve multiple legacy modules, manual reconciliations, inconsistent account status updates, and delays in GL posting visibility. A unified and governed data/functional view is essential for operational efficiency, regulatory compliance, and audit readiness.
3. Business Objectives
Establish a standardized Core Banking data model aligned with the bank's operational workflow.
Centralize customer, account, balance, transaction, fee, and GL data.
Ensure accurate, real-time and historical visibility of all customer and account activities.
Support regulatory, financial, and reconciliation reporting.
Reduce manual dependencies and improve process transparency.
4. Scope
In Scope:
Customer master & contact management
Account creation, modification, status lifecycle tracking
Capturing and storing financial transactions
Maintaining ledger and available balances
Fee computation and fee assessment
KYC profiling and periodic review tracking
General Ledger (GL) postings derived from transactions and fees
Out of Scope:
Loan origination workflows
Card systems
Wealth management or investment products
CRM segmentation logic beyond core identifiers
Treasury, market risk, or payments switching
5. Stakeholders
Retail Banking Operations
Customer Service & Branch Users
Finance & GL/Accounting Teams
Compliance & KYC Teams
Risk & Monitoring Teams
Data Engineering / Core Banking IT
Audit & Regulatory Reporting
6. Current State Challenges
Customer data inconsistencies across channels
Multiple representations of account status without event history
Manual KYC review tracking
Lack of unified view of transactions and balances
Delayed fee and GL posting visibility
High dependency on manual reconciliations
7. Future State Requirements
A unified data store linking customers, accounts, KYC, balances, transactions, and GL entries.
Event-driven account status lifecycle tracking.
Automated generation of GL postings for all monetary transactions and fee charges.
Real-time or near real-time balance updates.
Standardized customer and account attributes.
Full audit trail for all monetary and non-monetary activities.
8. Functional Requirements
8.1 Customer & KYC
System shall store core customer attributes including name, gender, DOB, and segment.
System shall store multiple customer contact details including phone, email, and address.
System shall maintain KYC profile including status, risk rating, last review and next review date.
System shall restrict customer onboarding unless minimum KYC criteria are met.
8.2 Account Management
System shall support creation of multiple account types (savings, current, deposit, etc.).
System shall track account currency, status, and ownership.
System shall store full account lifecycle status events (open, freeze, suspend, close, etc.).
System shall not allow transactions on closed or restricted accounts.
8.3 Transactions Processing
System shall store every transaction with timestamp, type, amount, and channel.
System shall support credit, debit, transfer, and adjustment transactions.
System shall update available balance and ledger balance in real time.
Each financial transaction shall create corresponding GL postings.
8.4 Balance Management
System shall maintain ledger and available balances for each account.
System shall maintain a timestamp for each balance snapshot.
System shall prevent transactions that exceed available balance where applicable.
8.5 Fees & Charges
System shall store fee definitions and transaction-level fee charges.
System shall apply fees based on fee type (maintenance, penalty, service fee, etc.).
System shall generate GL postings for all fee charges.
8.6 GL Posting & Accounting
System shall maintain mapping between transaction types and GL codes.
Every transaction and fee charge shall produce debit/credit GL entries.
System shall prevent unbalanced GL postings.
9. Reporting Requirements
Customer 360 view including customer profile, contact, KYC, and accounts.
Account 360 view including balance, status, transactions, and fees.
Account lifecycle status events reporting.
Daily transaction summaries by channel, type, and amount.
Fee revenue reporting by fee type and account.
GL trial balance alignment and exception monitoring.
KYC due-for-review reporting.
10. Data Requirements (Based on ER Diagram)
CUSTOMER: customer_id (PK), first_name, last_name, gender, date_of_birth, segment
CUSTOMER_CONTACT: customer_id (FK), mobile_number, email_id, address, city, country
ACCOUNT: account_id (PK), customer_id (FK), account_type, currency, account_status
ACCOUNT_STATUS_EVENT: status_event_id (PK), account_id (FK), old_status, new_status, change_timestamp
ACCOUNT_BALANCE: balance_id (PK), account_id (FK), as_of_timestamp, ledger_balance, available_balance
TRANSACTION: transaction_id (PK), account_id (FK), timestamp, type, amount, channel
FEE_CHARGE: fee_id (PK), account_id (FK), fee_type, fee_amount, assessment_date
KYC_PROFILE: kyc_id (PK), customer_id (FK), kyc_status, risk_rating, last_review_date, next_due_date
GL_ACCOUNT: gl_account_code (PK), gl_account_name, gl_type
GL_POSTING: gl_posting_id (PK), transaction_id (FK), gl_account_code (FK), dc_indicator, amount
11. Business Rules
A customer may have multiple accounts; an account belongs to one customer.
Each account may have multiple transactions and balance snapshots.
Each financial transaction must create two GL entries (Debit & Credit) unless exception-approved.
Fee charges must not be applied to closed or inactive accounts.
KYC must be reviewed before the next_due_date.
Account status should always reflect the latest status event.
12. Non-Functional Requirements
Performance: Transaction and balance updates must be near real time.
Scalability: Must support millions of customers and accounts.
Security: Role-based access, PII data masking.
Auditability: Full traceability from GL entries to original transactions.
Availability: 24/7 system availability with maintenance windows.
Data Quality: Referential integrity and data completeness checks.
13. Assumptions & Dependencies
Upstream channels provide clean customer and transaction data.
KYC classification rules are provided by Compliance.
GL mapping tables are maintained by Finance.
Real-time transaction feeds are available for balance updates.
14. Risks
Poor data quality may impact reconciliation.
Missing KYC reviews may cause regulatory penalties.
Incorrect GL mapping may cause financial misstatements.
High transaction volumes may impact system performance.
15. Acceptance Criteria
All core entities (Customer, Account, Transaction, Balances, Fees, GL) are implemented.
Full data lineage is documented.
Reporting outputs reconcile with legacy systems.
GL entries balance for every transaction and fee event.
KYC & account status workflows validated by Compliance & Ops.
Conceptual draft generated. Review the conceptual ER and use the same /orchestrate endpoint with artifact_id to update relationships or send 'approve' to continue.

conceptual.title: Core Banking Conceptual Data Model
conceptual.scope: Customer, loan, risk rating, facility, servicing account, credit assessment, provision, default event, and delinquency monitoring concepts only.

conceptual.rag_context_used:
Business concept: Customer. Canonical table: CUSTOMER. Definition: A borrower or counterparty who receives credit from the institution. Business purpose: Represents the primary party against whom credit exposure is established. Notes: One customer can have one or more facilities.
Business concept: Loan. Canonical table: LOAN. Definition: An individual drawdown or funded exposure created under a facility. Business purpose: Tracks the specific credit amount disbursed, pricing, and lifecycle status. Notes: A facility can generate one or more loans.
Business concept: Risk Rating. Canonical table: RISK_RATING. Definition: The internal credit grade assigned as an outcome of a credit assessment. Business purpose: Used to classify borrower or facility risk and support downstream risk processes. Notes: One assessment produces one rating record in this model.
Table summary: CUSTOMER represents Customer. Primary key: customer_id. Business attributes: customer_name means display name of the borrower or counterparty; customer_type means segment of the customer such as Retail, SME, or Corporate; date_of_birth means date of birth of the customer. Examples: customer_id example C00001; customer_name example Aarav Sharma; customer_type example Retail.
Table summary: LOAN represents Loan. Primary key: loan_id. Foreign keys: facility_id references FACILITY.facility_id. Business attributes: loan_amount means amount disbursed for the individual loan; interest_rate means contractual pricing rate associated with the loan; disbursement_date means date on which the loan amount was funded or made available; loan_status means lifecycle status of the loan. Examples: loan_id example L00001; facility_id example F00001; loan_amount example 650000.
Table summary: RISK_RATING represents Risk Rating. Primary key: risk_rating_id. Foreign keys: assessment_id references CREDIT_ASSESSMENT.assessment_id. Business attributes: rating_code means internal grade assigned to the assessed facility; rating_description means text description of the assigned internal grade; rating_date means date on which the rating became effective. Examples: risk_rating_id example R00001; assessment_id example A00001; rating_code example BBB.
Business concept: Provision. Canonical table: PROVISION. Definition: A period-end expected or recognized credit loss amount for a facility. Business purpose: Supports accounting and risk reporting by recording loss allowance over time. Notes: Includes stage-based provision amounts in this model.
Business concept: Loan Account. Canonical table: LOAN_ACCOUNT. Definition: The operational servicing account associated with a loan. Business purpose: Used to manage balances, repayments, and account-level servicing activity. Notes: Typically one operational account per loan in this model.
Business concept: Default Event. Canonical table: DEFAULT_EVENT. Definition: A formal record that a loan has entered default status. Business purpose: Marks a significant credit deterioration or failure to meet repayment obligations. Notes: A loan may or may not have a default event in this model.
Table summary: CREDIT_ASSESSMENT represents Credit Assessment. Primary key: assessment_id. Foreign keys: facility_id references FACILITY.facility_id. Business attributes: assessment_date means date on which the assessment was performed; assessment_type means type of assessment such as Initial, Annual Review, or Ad-hoc Review; assessed_by means credit analyst or risk officer responsible for the assessment. Examples: assessment_id example A00001; facility_id example F00001; assessment_date example 2024-01-05.
Business concept: Facility. Canonical table: FACILITY. Definition: A sanctioned credit arrangement approved for a customer, such as a term loan, overdraft, or working capital line. Business purpose: Acts as the contractual credit agreement and parent container for one or more loans or drawdowns. Notes: Central entity in the loan model.
Business concept: Delinquency Days (DPD). Canonical table: LOAN_MONITORING. Definition: The number of days a payment obligation is overdue. Business purpose: Used to monitor arrears and identify early warning signs or default risk. Notes: Also referred to as Days Past Due.

entities.name: Customer
entities.description: A borrower or counterparty who receives credit from the institution.

entities.name: Facility
entities.description: A sanctioned credit arrangement approved for a customer, such as a term loan, overdraft, or working capital line.

entities.name: Loan
entities.description: An individual drawdown or funded exposure created under a facility.

entities.name: Loan Account
entities.description: The operational servicing account associated with a loan, used to manage balances, repayments, and account-level servicing activity.

entities.name: Credit Assessment
entities.description: An assessment performed to evaluate a facility and support risk decisioning.

entities.name: Risk Rating
entities.description: The internal credit grade assigned as an outcome of a credit assessment.

entities.name: Provision
entities.description: A period-end expected or recognized credit loss amount for a facility.

entities.name: Default Event
entities.description: A formal record that a loan has entered default status.

entities.name: Delinquency Monitoring
entities.description: Monitoring of overdue payment obligations to track days past due and early warning signs of credit deterioration.

relationships.description: A customer may have one or more facilities.
relationships.label: holds

relationships.description: A facility can generate one or more loans.
relationships.label: generates

relationships.description: Each loan is associated with one operational servicing account.
relationships.label: serviced_by

relationships.description: A facility may be evaluated by one or more credit assessments over time.
relationships.label: is_assessed_by

relationships.description: Each assessment produces one risk rating record in this model.
relationships.label: results_in

relationships.description: A facility may have one or more provision records over time.
relationships.label: has

relationships.description: A loan may or may not have a default event in this model.
relationships.label: may_trigger

relationships.description: A loan may be monitored for delinquency across time.
relationships.label: is_monitored_by

business_rules: A customer can have one or more facilities.
A facility can generate one or more loans.
Each loan is associated with one operational servicing account.
One assessment produces one risk rating record in this model.
A loan may or may not have a default event in this model.
conceptual.conceptual_summary: This conceptual model represents the loan-focused core banking glossary domain. Customer is the primary party, Facility is the sanctioned credit arrangement, and Loan is the funded exposure created under a facility. The model also includes Loan Account for servicing, Credit Assessment and Risk Rating for credit evaluation, Provision for expected or recognized credit loss, Default Event for formal default recognition, and Delinquency Monitoring for days-past-due tracking.
conceptual.diagram_description: Customer connects to Facility in a one-to-many relationship. Facility connects to Loan in a one-to-many relationship and to Credit Assessment in a one-to-many relationship. Credit Assessment produces one Risk Rating. Loan connects to Loan Account in a one-to-one servicing relationship. Facility connects to Provision over time. Loan may connect to Default Event and to Delinquency Monitoring for performance tracking.

state.agent_final_answer: Conceptual draft generated. Review the conceptual ER and use the same /orchestrate endpoint with artifact_id to update relationships or send 'approve' to continue.

8.3 Transactions Processing
System shall store every transaction with timestamp, type, amount, and channel.
System shall support credit, debit, transfer, and adjustment transactions.
System shall update available balance and ledger balance in real time.
Each financial transaction shall create corresponding GL postings.
8.4 Balance Management
System shall maintain ledger and available balances for each account.
System shall maintain a timestamp for each balance snapshot.
System shall prevent transactions that exceed available balance where applicable.
8.5 Fees & Charges
System shall store fee definitions and transaction-level fee charges.
System shall apply fees based on fee type (maintenance, penalty, service fee, etc.).
System shall generate GL postings for all fee charges.
8.6 GL Posting & Accounting
System shall maintain mapping between transaction types and GL codes.
Every transaction and fee charge shall produce debit/credit GL entries.
System shall prevent unbalanced GL postings.
9. Reporting Requirements
Customer 360 view including customer profile, contact, KYC, and accounts.
Account 360 view including balance, status, transactions, and fees.
Account lifecycle status events reporting.
Daily transaction summaries by channel, type, and amount.
Fee revenue reporting by fee type and account.
GL trial balance alignment and exception monitoring.
KYC due-for-review reporting.
10. Data Requirements (Based on ER Diagram)
CUSTOMER: customer_id (PK), first_name, last_name, gender, date_of_birth, segment
CUSTOMER_CONTACT: customer_id (FK), mobile_number, email_id, address, city, country
ACCOUNT: account_id (PK), customer_id (FK), account_type, currency, account_status
ACCOUNT_STATUS_EVENT: status_event_id (PK), account_id (FK), old_status, new_status, change_timestamp
ACCOUNT_BALANCE: balance_id (PK), account_id (FK), as_of_timestamp, ledger_balance, available_balance
TRANSACTION: transaction_id (PK), account_id (FK), timestamp, type, amount, channel
FEE_CHARGE: fee_id (PK), account_id (FK), fee_type, fee_amount, assessment_date
KYC_PROFILE: kyc_id (PK), customer_id (FK), kyc_status, risk_rating, last_review_date, next_due_date
GL_ACCOUNT: gl_account_code (PK), gl_account_name, gl_type
GL_POSTING: gl_posting_id (PK), transaction_id (FK), gl_account_code (FK), dc_indicator, amount
11. Business Rules
A customer may have multiple accounts; an account belongs to one customer.
Each account may have multiple transactions and balance snapshots.
Each financial transaction must create two GL entries (Debit & Credit) unless exception-approved.
Fee charges must not be applied to closed or inactive accounts.
KYC must be reviewed before the next_due_date.
Account status should always reflect the latest status event.
12. Non-Functional Requirements
Performance: Transaction and balance updates must be near real time.
Scalability: Must support millions of customers and accounts.
Security: Role-based access, PII data masking.
Auditability: Full traceability from GL entries to original transactions.
Availability: 24/7 system availability with maintenance windows.
Data Quality: Referential integrity and data completeness checks.
13. Assumptions & Dependencies
Upstream channels provide clean customer and transaction data.
KYC classification rules are provided by Compliance.
GL mapping tables are maintained by Finance.
Real-time transaction feeds are available for balance updates.
14. Risks
Poor data quality may impact reconciliation.
Missing KYC reviews may cause regulatory penalties.
Incorrect GL mapping may cause financial misstatements.
High transaction volumes may impact system performance.
15. Acceptance Criteria
All core entities (Customer, Account, Transaction, Balances, Fees, GL) are implemented.
Full data lineage is documented.
Reporting outputs reconcile with legacy systems.
GL entries balance for every transaction and fee event.
KYC & account status workflows validated by Compliance & Ops.
model_json.rag_context_used:
Business concept: Customer. Canonical table: CUSTOMER. Definition: A borrower or counterparty who receives credit from the institution. Business purpose: Represents the primary party against whom credit exposure is established. Notes: One customer can have one or more facilities.
Business concept: Loan. Canonical table: LOAN. Definition: An individual drawdown or funded exposure created under a facility. Business purpose: Tracks the specific credit amount disbursed, pricing, and lifecycle status. Notes: A facility can generate one or more loans.
Business concept: Risk Rating. Canonical table: RISK_RATING. Definition: The internal credit grade assigned as an outcome of a credit assessment. Business purpose: Used to classify borrower or facility risk and support downstream risk processes. Notes: One assessment produces one rating record in this model.
Table summary: CUSTOMER represents Customer. Primary key: customer_id. Business attributes: customer_name means display name of the borrower or counterparty; customer_type means segment of the customer such as Retail, SME, or Corporate; date_of_birth means date of birth of the customer. Examples: customer_id example C00001; customer_name example Aarav Sharma; customer_type example Retail.
Table summary: LOAN represents Loan. Primary key: loan_id. Foreign keys: facility_id references FACILITY.facility_id. Business attributes: loan_amount means amount disbursed for the individual loan; interest_rate means contractual pricing rate associated with the loan; disbursement_date means date on which the loan amount was funded or made available; loan_status means lifecycle status of the loan. Examples: loan_id example L00001; facility_id example F00001; loan_amount example 650000.
Table summary: RISK_RATING represents Risk Rating. Primary key: risk_rating_id. Foreign keys: assessment_id references CREDIT_ASSESSMENT.assessment_id. Business attributes: rating_code means internal grade assigned to the assessed facility; rating_description means text description of the assigned internal grade; rating_date means date on which the rating became effective. Examples: risk_rating_id example R00001; assessment_id example A00001; rating_code example BBB.
Business concept: Provision. Canonical table: PROVISION. Definition: A period-end expected or recognized credit loss amount for a facility. Business purpose: Supports accounting and risk reporting by recording loss allowance over time. Notes: Includes stage-based provision amounts in this model.
Business concept: Loan Account. Canonical table: LOAN_ACCOUNT. Definition: The operational servicing account associated with a loan. Business purpose: Used to manage balances, repayments, and account-level servicing activity. Notes: Typically one operational account per loan in this model.
Business concept: Default Event. Canonical table: DEFAULT_EVENT. Definition: A formal record that a loan has entered default status. Business purpose: Marks a significant credit deterioration or failure to meet repayment obligations. Notes: A loan may or may not have a default event in this model.
Table summary: CREDIT_ASSESSMENT represents Credit Assessment. Primary key: assessment_id. Foreign keys: facility_id references FACILITY.facility_id. Business attributes: assessment_date means date on which the assessment was performed; assessment_type means type of assessment such as Initial, Annual Review, or Ad-hoc Review; assessed_by means credit analyst or risk officer responsible for the assessment. Examples: assessment_id example A00001; facility_id example F00001; assessment_date example 2024-01-05.
Business concept: Facility. Canonical table: FACILITY. Definition: A sanctioned credit arrangement approved for a customer, such as a term loan, overdraft, or working capital line. Business purpose: Acts as the contractual credit agreement and parent container for one or more loans or drawdowns. Notes: Central entity in the loan model.
Business concept: Delinquency Days (DPD). Canonical table: LOAN_MONITORING. Definition: The number of days a payment obligation is overdue. Business purpose: Used to monitor arrears and identify early warning signs or default risk. Notes: Also referred to as Days Past Due.

entities.name: Customer
entities.description: A borrower or counterparty who receives credit from the institution.

entities.name: Facility
entities.description: A sanctioned credit arrangement approved for a customer, such as a term loan, overdraft, or working capital line.

entities.name: Loan
entities.description: An individual drawdown or funded exposure created under a facility.

entities.name: Loan Account
entities.description: The operational servicing account associated with a loan, used to manage balances, repayments, and account-level servicing activity.

entities.name: Credit Assessment
entities.description: An assessment performed to evaluate a facility and support risk decisioning.

entities.name: Risk Rating
entities.description: The internal credit grade assigned as an outcome of a credit assessment.

entities.name: Provision
entities.description: A period-end expected or recognized credit loss amount for a facility.

entities.name: Default Event
entities.description: A formal record that a loan has entered default status.

entities.name: Delinquency Monitoring
entities.description: Monitoring of overdue payment obligations to track days past due and early warning signs of credit deterioration.

relationships.description: A customer may have one or more facilities.
relationships.label: holds

relationships.description: A facility can generate one or more loans.
relationships.label: generates

relationships.description: Each loan is associated with one operational servicing account.
relationships.label: serviced_by

relationships.description: A facility may be evaluated by one or more credit assessments over time.
relationships.label: is_assessed_by

relationships.description: Each assessment produces one risk rating record in this model.
relationships.label: results_in

relationships.description: A facility may have one or more provision records over time.
relationships.label: has

relationships.description: A loan may or may not have a default event in this model.

relationships.description: A loan may be monitored for delinquency across time.
relationships.label: is_monitored_by

business_rules: A customer can have one or more facilities.
A facility can generate one or more loans.
Each loan is associated with one operational servicing account.
One assessment produces one risk rating record in this model.
A loan may or may not have a default event in this model.
model_json.conceptual_summary: This conceptual model represents the loan-focused core banking glossary domain. Customer is the primary party, Facility is the sanctioned credit arrangement, and Loan is the funded exposure created under a facility. The model also includes Loan Account for servicing, Credit Assessment and Risk Rating for credit evaluation, Provision for expected or recognized credit loss, Default Event for formal default recognition, and Delinquency Monitoring for days-past-due tracking.
model_json.diagram_description: Customer connects to Facility in a one-to-many relationship. Facility connects to Loan in a one-to-many relationship and to Credit Assessment in a one-to-many relationship. Credit Assessment produces one Risk Rating. Loan connects to Loan Account in a one-to-one servicing relationship. Facility connects to Provision over time. Loan may connect to Default Event and to Delinquency Monitoring for performance tracking.
